options.A: Simple pocket, compound pocket, complex pocket.
options.B: Simple pocket, complex pocket, compound pocket.
options.C: Compound pocket, simple pocket, complex pocket.
options.D: Simple pocket, compound pocket, tertiary pocket.

history.message:
Agent1 Round 1: Simple pockets occur when there is an accumulation of fluid or debris beneath a flap without involving deeper structures. Complex pockets involve multiple layers of tissue including muscle fascia, while compound pockets may also include underlying bone involvement. The correct order based on complexity should be from least to greatest as A (simple - complex - compound).
Answer: A
